info.name: Tablix
info.description:
Tablix REST API — database discovery and query platform.

Set the `baseUrl` variable to your Tablix server (default: http://localhost:9100).
Set the `apiKey` variable to a valid API key from tablix.json (default: tablixadmin).

Swagger UI is available at {{baseUrl}}/swagger.

request.description: Returns server name, version, start time, and uptime. No authentication required.
Lightweight health check. Returns 200 OK with no body. No authentication required.

query.description: Filter by database ID or name (case-insensitive)

request.description: List all configured databases with pagination and optional filtering.
Get database details including connection settings, schema geometry (tables, columns, PKs, FKs, indexes), and crawl status.

body.raw:
{
  "Id": "db_new_database",
  "Name": "My New Database",
  "Type": "Postgresql",
  "Hostname": "localhost",
  "Port": 5432,
  "User": "postgres",
  "Password": "secret",
  "DatabaseName": "mydb",
  "Schema": "public",
  "AllowedQueries": ["SELECT"],
  "Context": "Description of this database for AI agents..."
}

request.description: Add a new database entry. An initial schema crawl is triggered automatically. Returns 201 Created on success, 409 Conflict if the ID already exists.
Update an existing database entry. The ID in the path is used; any ID in the body is ignored. The crawl cache is updated immediately.
Update only the user-supplied database context. Mode may be replace or append.
Generate database context from the last successful crawl using a configured chat provider, persist it to tablix.json, and update running settings.
Delete a database entry and remove it from the crawl cache. Returns 204 No Content on success.

Re-crawl the database schema. Discovers tables, columns, primary keys, foreign keys, and indexes. Returns the updated DatabaseDetail. If the crawl fails (unreachable host, bad credentials), the response still returns 200 with IsCrawled: false and a CrawlError message.

Re-crawl the database schema and stream status as server-sent events. Emits started, progress, and one terminal completed or failed event. Terminal events include table counts and the final DatabaseDetail.

query.description: Filter by table or schema name

request.description: List crawled database tables with pagination. Use NextSkip while EndOfResults is false.

query.description: Filter by table, column, schema, or constraint name
Filter by source or target schema
Reserved for inferred relationships; currently returns declared FKs only

request.description: List compact declared relationship edges with pagination.

Execute a SQL query against a database. The query must be a single statement (no semicolons) and the statement type must be in the database's AllowedQueries list. Returns 403 if the statement type is not allowed, 500 if execution fails (with the error in the Description field).
Returns enabled chat providers and configured databases for the dashboard Chat page. Provider API keys are redacted.
Sends a non-streaming database-aware chat request through the selected PolyPrompt provider.
Streams chat tokens and final telemetry as server-sent events.
Returns redacted form-editable server settings. Provider API keys are represented by HasApiKey only.

body.raw:
{
  "Rest": {
    "Hostname": "*",
    "Port": 9100,
    "Ssl": false,
    "McpPort": 9102
  },
  "Logging": {
    "Servers": [],
    "ConsoleLogging": true,
    "FileLogging": true,
    "LogDirectory": "./logs/",
    "LogFilename": "tablix.log",
    "MinimumSeverity": 0,
    "EnableColors": true
  },
  "ApiKeys": [
    "tablixadmin"
  ],
  "Chat": {
    "Enabled": true,
    "DefaultProviderId": "provider_ollama_local",
    "DefaultStreaming": true,
    "SystemPrompt": "You are Tablix, a database assistant. Restrict your conversation to only the selected database, its structure, its contents, and their relationships. Use configured database context first, inspect schema before writing SQL, run only allowed query types, clearly label inferred relationships, and never reveal credentials or secret settings.",
    "MaxContextTables": 100,
    "Tools": {
      "Enabled": true,
      "AllowReadOnlyQueries": true,
      "AllowContextUpdates": true,
      "MaxToolIterations": 8,
      "MaxToolCalls": 20,
      "ToolTimeoutMs": 30000,
      "MaxToolOutputCharacters": 12000
    },
    "Providers": [
      {
        "Id": "provider_ollama_local",
        "Name": "Local Ollama",
        "Type": "Ollama",
        "Endpoint": "http://ollama:11434",
        "ApiKey": null,
        "ClearApiKey": false,
        "Model": "gemma3:4b",
        "SystemPrompt": null,
        "Enabled": true,
        "DefaultStreaming": true,
        "Temperature": 0.2,
        "TopP": null,
        "MaxTokens": 4096,
        "RequestTimeoutMs": 120000
      }
    ]
  }
}

request.description: Updates running settings and persists tablix.json. Leave provider ApiKey null/empty to preserve it; ClearApiKey removes it.
